title: NIH Research Matters | National Institutes of Health (NIH)
body: NIH Research Matters April 28, 2026 Edition AI tool predicts patients at risk of intimate partner violence A new artificial intelligence tool may help health care providers identify patients at risk of intimate partner violence and provide early interventions. Weakened gut-brain connection may contribute to memory loss Boosting the immune response to brain cancer SuperAgers show unique cell signatures in the brain March 31, 2026 — Researchers linked neuron creation to exceptional recall and memory in older adults. Scientists identify proteins tied to food tolerance March 31, 2026 — New research enhances our understanding of food tolerance and may lead to new therapies for people with food allergies. Using RNA to treat heart attacks March 31, 2026 — An NIH-supported research team developed a minimally invasive therapy that may help the heart recover from a heart attack. Research in Context Treating addiction March 24, 2026 — Alcohol and drug addiction can cause many harms. This Research in Context feature looks at research into the causes of addiction and new ways to treat it. Blood test predicts start of Alzheimer’s disease symptoms March 17, 2026 — Scientists developed an Alzheimer’s “clock” that can roughly predict when symptoms of Alzheimer’s disease will arise. Machine learning analysis of CT scans March 17, 2026 — An AI-powered tool can interpret 3D images from CT scans and diagnose certain disorders. Preventing organ transplant rejection March 17, 2026 — Researchers found that the body’s lymphatic drainage system may play an unexpected role in chronic organ rejection. Immune cells may underlie sex differences in chronic pain March 10, 2026 — A study in mice found that certain immune cells may be a key reason why chronic pain is more common in women than men. 3D human liver reconstruction reveals changes in cirrhosis March 10, 2026 — A new 3D reconstruction of human liver tissue revealed changes to the liver’s internal structure in liver disease. Engineered immune cells target Alzheimer’s disease protein March 10, 2026 — Scientists designed a T cell that reduced signs of disease in the brains of a mouse model for Alzheimer’s disease. Search NIH Research Matters Popular Stories ADHD medications stimulate alertness, motivation Blood test predicts start of Alzheimer’s disease symptoms Protective vitamin shots for newborns on decline Bacteria play key role in kidney stones One dose of HPV vaccine as effective as two Research in Context In-depth articles that focus on cutting-edge NIH biomedical research topics, describing the current state of research in a field and where it may be heading. View all stories Connect with Us RSS feed Email us Subscribe Mailing Address: NIH Research Matters National Institutes of Health 31 Center Drive, MSC 2094 Bethesda, MD 20892-2094 About NIH Research Matters Editor: Harrison Wein, Ph.D. Assistant Editors: Vicki Contie and Brian Doctrow, Ph.D. NIH Research Matters is a weekly update of NIH research highlights reviewed by NIH’s experts. It's published by the Office of Communications and Public Liaison in the NIH Office of the Director . ISSN 2375-9593 This page last reviewed on April 15, 2025
